name: Amazonas (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the sky over Amazonas; appears on the canton

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of white, red and white, with a blue canton in the upper hoist bearing a large central white five-pointed star surrounded by 25 smaller white stars.
history: Adopted on 18 September 1897 by State Decree No. 165, designed under Governor Eduardo Ribeiro; the 25 small stars represent the municipalities of the state at the time, and the larger central star represents Amazonas itself.

features.symbolism: Three equal horizontal stripes of white, red and white, recalling the colours of liberty and republican ideals
Blue canton bearing one large central star surrounded by 25 small stars

features.role: the municipalities at the time of adoption
features.symbolism: the 25 municipalities of Amazonas at the time the flag was adopted
